Intro

On many accounts, and now against the instincts of my youth, I consider myself a relatively average person who will live a decent-to-good life and then succumb to obscurity. This is the meditative peace I've found myself in—imagining myself happy in my own corner of the world. This is not to say I am without passion, or that I wouldn't seize an opportunity to reach a greater spotlight, but always in the back of my mind, I believe in my own mundanity. Even if I became a name hailed for some grand innovation or accomplishment, in the grand scheme of things—even within my generation—I would still fall within a minute percentage, with peers towering greater and higher than I do. There is a coziness to this knowledge.

I am passionate about many things, and these passions fluctuate a great deal. I have a fascination for systems, specifically human systems: the infrastructure that allows our lives to function; the small ways our bodies, minds, and speech patterns have evolved for particular aims; the wars we wage to allow for the trade of goods that produce our comfort. Everything is connected to lead to this moment, and to the moments after, and after, and after. This is perhaps where my idea of my mundanity stems from: this mundane world, by my inquiry, is already profoundly fascinating enough, and I've come to this generation with great luck.

I'm interested in tech, especially SFF technology. I'm also into History, with some knowledge of ancient Mesopotamia and Classical Greece. As for gaming, I enjoy Minecraft and Cyberpunk 2077. For Philosophy, I'm fond of Hume, Lacan, and Nietzsche; though generally I gravitate toward post-modernists, epistemologists, and existentialist philosophers.

Right now, since the start of this year, I've been studying Chinese. As of writing this post, I'm currently learning HSK2 and polishing my HSK1.

I am not a particularly good person; though I'm not a particularly evil one either. But I wanted to claim the former first—perhaps as confession. The details I won't delve into here, and it's stated mostly in earnest as a precaution.

The Mind →

I wish to detail my way of thinking, beliefs and personality here. It will not be in any way rigorous, systemic or complete; and, by necessity, will be filled with bias. There is also a stark difference between the person I was and the person I am, and yet I am still informed by such difference and I find it necessary to talk about it. I also understand that when I get older I will undoubtedly change yet again.

On the briefest explanations of my personality, I am typically laid-back and perhaps, to many, non-chalant; but I do mean well in general and try to get along socially when I can. I enjoy talking about and rambling on about some thing, thought or knowledge I have; and being fascinated by minor things and sharing details about worldbuilding projects and the likes. I believe myself socially competent, albeit aloof enough that this competence isn't so reliable.

Now in regards to my mind, I am someone who thinks, backtracks, circles around, and abruptly shifts gears, but still I have a tendency to make sure I maintain logical continuity. I rapidly change frameworks in my mind to try different means of attacks, and my attention is quick to be shifted (though I have enough self-control to be effective and competent in my professional life).

If this isn't enough for you, click the name of this section.

Technology

  • Cambridge Melomania M100
